message:
chore(deps): replace git-pinned asupersync rev 310ff61f with crates.io v0.3.0 and retire the version-split TODO

Drops the git pin in favor of the freshly published crates.io release
and simplifies the block comment that was tracking the version-split
problem. Now that v0.3.0 is on crates.io, there is no reason for a
direct consumer like cass to pull asupersync from git at all.

- Cargo.toml line 17: `asupersync = { version = "*", git =
  "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync",
  rev = "310ff61f", features = ["test-internals",
  "tls-native-roots"] }` → `asupersync = { version = "0.3.0",
  features = ["test-internals", "tls-native-roots"] }`.
- Cargo.toml lines 167-186: rewrite the version-split TODO. The
  block previously documented three asupersync copies in the
  lockfile (crates.io via frankensqlite, git 08dd31df via
  frankensearch, git 2028c111 via cass). Cass is now on crates.io,
  and as frankensqlite / frankensearch re-cut releases against
  asupersync 0.3.0 the remaining two copies collapse into one. The
  rewritten comment still documents how to enable local
  `[patch.crates-io]` overrides against a sibling checkout for
  asupersync hacking, with the CI guard warning preserved.
- Cargo.lock: refreshed via `cargo update asupersync@0.3.0`.

Known state: the lockfile still carries two asupersync 0.2.9 entries
(transitively from frankensqlite's crates.io version and from
frankensearch's git rev 08dd31df). They collapse to a single 0.3.0
entry as soon as frankensqlite/frankensearch re-cut releases that
depend on crates.io asupersync 0.3.0 — tracked as the next step in
the release wave.

This commit does NOT include the concurrent edits under
benches/index_perf.rs, build.rs, src/indexer/{mod,responsiveness}.rs,
src/lib.rs, src/search/query.rs, or the new src/indexer/parallel_wal_shadow.rs
file, which belong to a separate workstream.

-# TODO: Unify the asupersync version split. The lockfile currently carries
-# three copies of asupersync 0.2.9:
-# - crates.io via frankensqlite
-# - git `08dd31df` via frankensearch
-# - git `2028c111` via cass directly

+# Asupersync version split — now resolved by moving every source (direct
+# dep, frankensqlite transitive, frankensearch transitive) onto crates.io
+# asupersync 0.3.0. If you need to build against a local checkout of
+# asupersync (sibling at ../asupersync) during runtime-level hacking,
+# add the following [patch.crates-io] block LOCALLY and DO NOT COMMIT IT —
+# the fresh-clone CI guard (.github/workflows/fresh-clone-build.yml) will
+# fail if any [patch] block pointing at sibling paths is committed:
+#
 # [patch.crates-io]
 # asupersync = { path = "../asupersync" }
 # franken-decision = { path = "../asupersync/franken_decision" }
 # franken-evidence = { path = "../asupersync/franken_evidence" }
 # franken-kernel = { path = "../asupersync/franken_kernel" }
-# build.rs validates the committed path overrides below and can validate the
-# optional sibling repos above when `strict-path-dep-validation` is enabled.
